birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The American black duck is endemic to eastern North America. In Canada, the range extends from northeastern Saskatchewan to Newfoundland and Labrador. In the United States, it is found in northern Illinois, Michigan, New Jersey, Ohio, Connecticut, Vermont, South Dakota, central West Virginia, Maine and on the Atlantic coast to North Carolina.{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=nTM3AQAAMAAJ&pg=PA83|title=Cape Cod National Seashore (N.S.), Hunting Program: Environmental Impact Statement|last=U.S
